title: Workbook — Confirmation
html:
Student Workbook

To use after the lesson or in a group. Print this page or work in your notebook.

1. Remembering the Essentials

  1. Define Confirmation in your own words: what is it and what is it for?
  2. How many gifts of the Holy Spirit are there? Name them and briefly explain what each one does.
  3. Why do we say that Confirmation is the “personal Pentecost” of the Christian?
  4. Who is the ordinary minister of Confirmation and why?
  5. At what age is Confirmation normally received in your diocese, and why do you think that age was chosen?

2. For Reflection and Writing

Answer each question honestly. There are no “right” answers to these personal questions.

1. If you have already received Confirmation, what was your preparation like? Describe it: what did you learn, what did you feel, who was your sponsor?

Do you remember the day of your Confirmation? What impressed you the most?

2. If you haven't received it yet, why do you want to? Is it your decision or your family's?

3. Of the seven gifts, which one do you think is already most developed in you, and which one do you most urgently need?

Gift most present in me: ________________________
Gift I most need: ________________________
Explain why.

4. Had you heard of St. José Sánchez del Río before? What stands out most to you about his martyrdom?

5. They say Confirmation isn't a “graduation” but a “commissioning.” What does this mean for you, in your concrete daily life?

3. Practical Activities

A. My Shield: Soldier of Christ

On a blank sheet of paper, draw a shield (like a medieval knight's) divided into seven sections, one for each gift of the Holy Spirit. In each section, write the name of the gift and draw a symbol that represents it. In the center of the shield, write “Holy Spirit” because he is the source of everything. At the bottom, write a personal battle motto (like “Long live Christ the King!” but in your own style).

B. Confirmation in the Bible

Read Acts 8:14-17 and Acts 19:1-7. In a notebook, answer:

  • Who lays on hands? Whom do they represent today?
  • What happens when they lay on hands? Is it the same as Baptism or something different?
  • How do these two passages relate to your Confirmation or the one you plan to receive?

C. Prayer to the Holy Spirit (Personalized)

Read and then rewrite this prayer in your own words, adding your personal petition at the end:

Come, Holy Spirit, fill the hearts of your faithful
and kindle in them the fire of your love.
Send forth your Spirit and they shall be created,
and you shall renew the face of the earth.

 

 

 

 

 

D. Letter to a Friend Who Left the Church

Imagine you have a friend who was confirmed at 15 and never returned to church afterward. Write them a brief letter (8-10 lines) in which, without judging, you remind them what they received at their Confirmation and invite them to rediscover it. Be warm, not preachy.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

4. Memory Verse

“You shall receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you;
and you shall be my witnesses in Jerusalem and in all Judea and Samaria
and to the end of the earth.”

— Acts 1:8

5. Prayer for This Week

Come, Holy Spirit,
you who anointed Jesus and sent him into the world.
Anoint me too with your strength.
Give me the gift of wisdom to seek only what is worth seeking,
the gift of fortitude not to give in to evil,
the gift of piety to treat you as a Father,
and the gift of fear of the Lord never to offend you.
Like St. José Sánchez del Río and St. Joan of Arc,
teach me to be a witness not in word alone, but in life.
Amen.

6. Weekly Challenge

Choose one of these three challenges for this week:

  • Fortitude Challenge: identify a situation in which you normally stay silent about your faith (mockery, hostile environments, embarrassment) and this week say something — respectfully but clearly — that shows you are a Christian.
  • Piety Challenge: spend 10 minutes a day talking with the Holy Spirit. It could be spontaneous prayer, reading a Scripture passage, or simply sitting in silence saying: “Speak, Lord, your servant is listening.”
  • Wisdom Challenge: this week, before every decision (even small ones), ask yourself: “Does this bring me closer to God or push me further away?” At the end of the week, write down what you discovered.
Print Workbook
